level_signals: Described as 'senior member of the Product Management team'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of product management experience, preferably working on platform products.
Highly analytical mindset, able to balance strategic thinking with a strong bias for action and hands-on problem-solving.
Proven track record of launching and managing high-impact products.
Outstanding written and verbal communication with a diverse stakeholder audience.
The ability to turn incomplete, conflicting, or ambiguous inputs into solid action plans.
Solid technical chops that allow you to have in depth technical discussions and opinions.
This position requires either equivalent practical experience or a Bachelor's degree in a related field

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of product management experience, preferably working on platform products.

scope.surface: Affirm's backend financial systems and data infrastructure; foundational platform capabilities powering financial workflows and integrations
primary_metrics_inferred: Integration velocity across engineering and business systems
core_responsibilities: Solve complex and ambiguous platform problems, define execution strategies that balance short and long-term impact, and drive operational improvements across critical financial systems.
Define product objectives and own the roadmap for foundational platform capabilities, integrations, and shared services that support financial operations and reporting workflows.
Partner closely with Engineering and Data teams to define APIs, data contracts, platform interfaces, and migration strategies that improve scalability, reliability, and maintainability.
Define product objectives, partner with engineering and cross-functional partners to drive requirements, ultimately lead execution to launch key products and features.
Regularly communicate across product and engineering teams as well as cross-functional stakeholders, including frequently presenting to senior leadership.

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade - 6; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan)

green_flags: 100% subsidized medical, dental, and vision coverage
Clearly scoped product surface (financial platforms / backend infrastructure)
Equity and ESPP mentioned as part of total compensation
Existing vacancy explicitly stated — active hiring need
red_flags: Compensation stated in CAN (Canadian dollars) — ambiguous whether this applies to US candidates or only Canadian; US base range not provided
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Pay grade ('N') and equity grade ('6') are internal Affirm classifications without external context

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, 'senior member' language, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred: JD mentions location is a factor in pay)
ambiguities: Compensation range is labeled 'CAN base pay range' — unclear if this is Canadian dollars for Canadian employees only, or if a US range was omitted. Treated as stated but flagged.

'4+ years of product management experience, preferably working on platform products' — 'preferably' qualifier makes the platform PM experience a soft preference, but the 4+ years PM is a hard requirement. Extracted as two separate items accordingly.
Location: JD footer says '#LI-Remote' and body describes remote-first, but also references proximal roles with occasional office requirements. Role appears fully remote based on context.
